title: Leclerc leads Miami FP1, Mercedes hit trouble early on
body:
The highly anticipated Miami Grand Prix got off to a thrilling start on Friday, with Ferrari's Charles Leclerc setting the pace in the first free practice session. The Monaco-born driver edged out Red Bull's Max Verstappen and McLaren's Oscar Piastri to top the timesheets, as the 2026 Formula 1 season resumed in style. With the sun shining down on the Miami circuit, the drivers took to the track with gusto, eager to make their mark on the opening day of the weekend's proceedings. As the session drew to a close, it was Leclerc who emerged victorious, his Ferrari handling the demands of the Miami track with ease.

As the session progressed, it became clear that the soft tire compound would be the key to unlocking the fastest times. With around 20 minutes remaining, a slew of drivers made the switch to the softer rubber, sparking a frantic flurry of activity as they sought to improve their lap times. Leclerc, Verstappen, and Piastri were among those to benefit from the switch, each driver shaving precious seconds off their previous best efforts. The resulting timesheets made for intriguing reading, with the top three drivers separated by just a handful of tenths of a second. With such a tightly packed field, the stage is set for a thrilling weekend of racing in Miami.

Meanwhile, it was a more challenging session for the Mercedes team, who faced early issues that hindered their progress. The Brackley-based outfit has been working tirelessly to address the shortcomings that have plagued them in recent weeks, but it appears they still have work to do. As their rivals continued to push the pace, Mercedes were left playing catch-up, their drivers struggling to extract the maximum performance from their cars. With the team's usual high standards, this early setback will undoubtedly be a cause for concern, and they will be eager to bounce back in the remaining practice sessions.

Despite the challenges faced by Mercedes, the overall pace of the field was impressive, with a number of drivers showing promising signs. Piastri, in particular, caught the eye with a strong showing, the Australian rookie demonstrating his growing confidence behind the wheel of his McLaren. As he continues to gain experience and develop his skills, Piastri is increasingly becoming a force to be reckoned with, and his performance in Miami will undoubtedly have raised a few eyebrows in the paddock. With a number of other drivers also showing well, the competition for the top spots is set to be fierce, and the Miami Grand Prix is shaping up to be a real thriller.

As the weekend unfolds, attention will turn to the remaining practice sessions, where the drivers will look to fine-tune their cars and prepare for the all-important qualifying session. With the grid still very much up for grabs, every lap will count, and the teams will be pushing hard to extract every last bit of performance from their cars. The Miami circuit, with its unique layout and demanding corners, will provide a stern test of both driver and machine, and only those who are truly on top of their game will emerge victorious.

As the Formula 1 circus heads into the remainder of the Miami Grand Prix weekend, the excitement is building, and the stage is set for a real showdown. With Leclerc and Verstappen already looking strong, and a host of other drivers waiting in the wings, the battle for supremacy is set to be intense. As the drivers take to the track once again, the question on everyone's lips will be: who will emerge victorious in Miami? Only time will tell, but one thing is certain – the next 48 hours are set to be a wild ride, full of twists and turns, and plenty of high-octane action.
